name: Clifford Jarvis
abstract: Clifford Osbourne Jarvis (August 26, 1941 – November 26, 1999) was an American hard bop and free jazz drummer, who in the 1980s moved to London, England, where he spent the remainder of his career.

concerts.label: Laura Nyro, The Rascals,  Alice Coltrane, and Pharoah Sanders

about_quotes.speaker: Wendell Harrison
about_quotes.evidence: He had another drummer, it might have been Phil Grant, I’m not sure, from Chicago. But I worked with him with Lex Humphries you know.
about_quotes.doc_title: Wendell Harrison interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 17, 2022 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: Freddie played there with Clifford Jarvis. Another great drummer Clifford Jarvis eventually wound up playing with Sun Ra...
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Barron
about_quotes.evidence: That’s where I think I heard Cecil Taylor for the first time, playing a duo with a trumpeter named Clifford Jarvis.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
